transcription:
CSE 138 · Introduction
Zulip is open-source discord
5th time teaching the course

[figure]

If you send a request,
and don't receive a
response, is impossible
to know why from inside the
system.

Timeout: After some specified time, if
no respose, give up and perform an action
No good way to pick a timeout length
What if the request that has a side effect?
No way of knowing the change-of-state
Byzantine Fault: Lie or interception
by malicious actor  ??
Assume Unbounded Latency:

Why distributed systems? (Have to deal with so much)
Some systems Machines crash and die,
so we can backup    (This will come up again
- Fault Tolerance        implications)
- Scalability
- Things are physically distributed in rl
- Data Locality
